title: anthriscus vulgaris var. trichosperma
intro: Anthriscus vulgaris var. trichosperma, commonly known as wild celery, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of wild celery include its ability to aid digestion, relieve flatulence, and support the immune system. Wild celery can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine.
